TextENGNew: The birdsong was pleasant.
It was a refreshing morning.
But that feeling lasted only a moment after I woke up.
changeReason: Old says "It's been a while since I last woke up feeling this way" (positive nostalgia); new says "But that feeling lasted only a moment after I woke up" (fleeting sensation). Japanese 束の間 means "fleeting/momentary".

TextENGNew: What Ooishi-san had told me about Mion yesterday quickly flooded my mind.
It was almost too much to believe.
A side of Mion I'd never known... No -- Mion's true face.
A face from the past, when she'd directed the radical wing of the dam opposition movement.
The face of the de facto leader of the Sonozaki family, with its vast underbelly of dark power.
changeReason: Old says "The dark, powerful face of the true leader"; new says "the de facto leader of the Sonozaki family, with its vast underbelly of dark power" -- different structural emphasis on the dark power.

TextENGNew: But before any of that, Mion was my friend.
I wanted so badly to keep believing that...
But did Mion feel the same way about me...?
To Mion, the face she showed as a 'school friend.'
changeReason: Old says "That she was my friend from school" (Keiichi's perspective); new says "To Mion, the face she showed as a school friend" (Mion's perspective). Japanese describes Mion's perspective.

TextENGNew: Maybe that was nothing more than a surface-level mask.
Her top priority should be the face she wore as head of the Sonozaki family.
changeReason: Old says "Maintaining her position...should be her top priority"; new says "Her top priority should be the face she wore as head" -- old is about maintaining position, new is about which face takes priority.

TextENGNew: It wouldn't be strange at all if she thought that way.
changeReason: Old says "When I thought about it that way, nothing seemed out of place"; new says "It wouldn't be strange at all if she thought that way" -- shifts from narrator's reflection to hypothetical about Mion.

TextENGNew: In fact, it would only be natural...
Going to school -- something so ordinary -- felt inexplicably heavy today.
Mion wasn't at our meeting spot, so Rena and I headed to school together.
I felt a twinge of anxiety about Mion not showing up.
But at the same time, I noticed a part of me was relieved... and that disgusted me.
Maybe a darkness in my attitude was starting to show through.
Rena seemed to pick up on it, because she didn't talk to me much.
She and I walked to school in near-total silence.
Even after arriving at school, the gloom hanging over me didn't lift.
Oh, right... Satoko's traps!
That might be just the change of pace I needed. Without hesitation, I threw open the classroom door.
There was no trap waiting for me. ...I should have been glad, but instead I felt oddly let down.
After exchanging quick greetings with everyone, I went to my desk and stayed put.
Somehow, the idea of making conversation with my friends felt like a chore.
My friends... and yet...
I put my head down and pretended to sleep.
I'd just ride it out like this until the teacher arrived.
I was running low on energy today.
On a day like this, surely pretending to sleep would be forgiven.
Just then --
``Morning, everyone~``
It was Mion, arriving after me.@k It seemed her cold had finally cleared up.
I was relieved to see her looking healthy, but the story Ooishi-san had told me instantly resurfaced.
How should I act around her...?
Since I'd been pretending to sleep when Mion arrived, there was no need to force a conversation this morning.
On the other hand,
if I didn't say hello now, it would only get harder to talk to her later.
I was torn between those two conflicting thoughts.
In the end, I kept up the sleeping act until the teacher arrived.
``Keiichi, the teacher is coming.``
I raised my head at Rika-chan's voice, suppressing my relief at being rescued.
I was grateful to Rika-chan for playing her part so well.
``...Ah, thanks, Rika-chan.@k@vS17/01/110100267. Really sleepy today~``
But as my eyes met Rika-chan's, I was about to smile back -- and froze.
Her eyes shone as if to say, 'I see right through you.'
``Good morning, Kei-chan.``
``...You look a bit gloomy. Are you okay?``
``I-I'm fine.@k@vS17/01/110100271. It's nothing.``
``...Is that so?``
Having suddenly greeted me, Mion said nothing more and returned to her seat.
I noticed a small wave of relief wash over me.@k Right now, I didn't trust myself to hold a conversation with Mion.
No -- the very fact that I was thinking 'I need to handle talking to Mion carefully' meant something was already wrong with me.
It wasn't Mion's fault...
If someone really was targeting me...
Then just come out and tell me already!
...No one was targeting me.
That's why nobody was telling me anything.
That possibility was perfectly plausible.
In fact, thinking about it rationally, that was the most sensible conclusion.
Was I just a paranoid mess, imagining threats where there were none...?
I couldn't keep going like this.
I needed to verify things for myself.
And yet... with Mion...
...Wait. What about Rika-chan?
From what I'd heard before, Rika-chan was the head of the Furude family, one of the Three Great Families of Hinamizawa.
Given her age, she might just be a figurehead.@k ...Even so, being close in age to Mion, there was a good chance she had access to the same level of information.
If anything unsettling was happening with the Sonozaki family behind Mion, or with the village at large, it wouldn't be surprising if word had reached her ears too... or so I figured.
And as things stood, Rika-chan was still friendly toward me.
However...
I remembered the look in Rika-chan's eyes from earlier.
That gaze, as if she could see through everything.
Rika-chan, whom the village elders believed possessed some kind of mysterious power...
What if Rika-chan had a hidden side, too...?
In the worst case, approaching her could be a dangerous move...
Even so, with my limited options, this was a valuable avenue I couldn't afford to pass up.
I would try asking Rika-chan.
Whether I was safe right now... or not...
I called Rika-chan out during a break.
Nobody saw the two of us leave. Perfect.
Rika-chan was looking at me with a quizzical expression.
Seeing that face calmed me down a little.
This was the usual Rika-chan.
``What do you need, Keiichi?``
``Yes?@k@vS17/05/110500048. Is this perhaps a sudden confession of love?``
``No, it's not that...``
``...Mii, how disappointing.@k@|@y@vS17/05/110500050. Then what is it?``
Seeing that I wasn't going to take the bait, Rika-chan tilted her head slightly and asked directly.
``Am I... being targeted by someone?``
``...Targeted?@k@vS17/05/110500052. You, Keiichi?``

``I've been hearing some rumors, and...@k@vS17/01/110100277. I was wondering if there might be something to them...@k@vS17/01/110100278. There are probably people in the village who still see me as an outsider...``
For an instant, Rika-chan's face registered something between surprise and anger, but it quickly settled into a neutral expression.
``...Keiichi, did you do something wrong?``
``N-no, I haven't!@k@vS17/01/110100280. I swear I haven't done anything wrong!``
``...Then you'll be fine.``
Rika-chan said it in a rather dismissive tone.
Was she angry?
Or was she simply exasperated?
``If you haven't done anything, then logically there's no reason to worry about anything happening to you.``
``...Y-yeah.@k@vS17/01/110100283. If that's the case, then good, but...``
``...You're being awfully persistent, Keiichi.``
Rika-chan clearly wanted to end the conversation.
Was I just being a nuisance?@k Or was it actually a mistake to bring this up with Rika-chan?
If it was the latter... then this had been a very dangerous move...
What should I do. What should I do. What should I do.
``--Keiichi.@k@vS17/05/110500058. Are you listening?``
``Y-yeah!@k@vS17/01/110100285. I'm listening! Yeah!``
It seemed she'd been calling my name for a while.
I'd heard that Rika-chan possessed some kind of mysterious, almost divine power.
If that were true, then she might be reading every thought in my head right now...
What should I do. What should I do. What should I do. What should I do. What should I do.
``I'll say it one more time, clearly.@k@vS17/05/110500060. Your name has never once come up at the village council meetings, Keiichi.``
...I-is that so?
``So there's absolutely nothing to be afraid of.@k@vS17/05/110500062. ...Is that enough?``
``...Th-thank you!@k@vS17/01/110100287. That really puts my mind at ease.``
Rika Furude, head of the Furude family -- one of the Three Great Families -- had told me I was safe.
If she meant it, then I was certainly safe.
And yet, something about Rika-chan still seemed off.@k There was an edge to her that wasn't usually there... something cold.
Driving the point home, Rika-chan spoke in a deeply displeased voice.

``...In the first place, Keiichi, what are you so afraid of?@k@vS17/05/110500064. Acting so strangely anxious is only going to make the dogs angrier for no reason...@k@vS17/05/110500065. Woof, woof woof.``
That sounded like the kind of thing the usual Rika-chan would say.
...But to me, right now, it sounded like thorns concealing a warning of danger.
``......I don't like seeing you this way, Keiichi.@k@vS17/05/110500067. So, goodbye.``
In the end, I couldn't say anything back...
Once again, I came to realize my actions had accomplished nothing.
If anything, there was even a chance I'd made things worse...
...I'd been running away.
...Running away from Mion.
Nothing would be resolved unless I talked to Mion directly.
In the worst case, it might even increase the danger I was in.
...But there was no other way.
I would talk to Mion, face to face...
After school. Mion apparently had a part-time job to get to, and she left the classroom alone.
The thought of being one-on-one with her made me a little tense.
But if we needed to talk privately, this was actually a perfect opportunity.
I went after Mion.
``Hey, Mion...``
Mion answered as if nothing were out of the ordinary.
She turned around, and our eyes met.
It hit me anew how long it had been since we'd faced each other like this.
I wonder if Mion felt the same?
``I was thinking we could walk home together...``
``Oh really... It's been a while since you've been the one to suggest it, Kei-chan~``
``I-I guess so...``
Amid the same old cicada chorus, along the same old path, we walked side by side for the first time in a while.
But Mion seemed different from before.
Her expression looked a bit stiff -- or was that just my imagination?
I'd managed to catch up with Mion, but...
What should I say?
What should I ask about first?
My thoughts were in total disarray.
Or rather, I simply couldn't bring myself to commit...
Our paths would diverge before long.
I needed to broach the subject quickly.
But that urgency turned out to be unnecessary.
Mion cut straight to the heart of the matter.
``...So, what is it?``
``What's what?``
``You chased after me because you had something to say.@k@vS17/03/110300264. ...Am I wrong?``
There was no reproach or barb in her voice, but it carried an air of resolve.
Don't waste my time -- that's what I heard.@k I had no choice but to be direct.
``Y-yeah.@k@vS17/01/110100293. Sharp as always.``
``So, what?``
``...Why did you keep the serial killings on Watanagashi night a secret for so long?``
I watched Mion's expression closely.@k How would she react...?
``...I didn't want to worry you.@k@vS17/03/110300267. You'd only just moved to Hinamizawa, Kei-chan.``
...I couldn't find anything suspicious in her explanation.
``...Then why did you decide to tell me after the festival?``
``I figured keeping quiet might actually make you more anxious.``
Same with this explanation.@k Again... nothing suspicious at all.
``In the end, whether I told you or kept quiet, it wasn't going to turn out well either way...``
Mion murmured that and gave a wry smile.
Had it all been my imagination after all?
Right now, Mion and I were having a perfectly normal conversation.
And Mion was giving me answers that were completely reasonable.
That's what I'd been thinking, but... then.@k Mion stopped walking and slowly turned to face me.
Her eyes bore into me with fierce intensity.
``--So, what are you going to do?@k@vS17/03/110300272. Keep your mouth shut like this... or?``
...Who was this person?
That was my first impression of the gaze boring into me from point-blank range.
The person standing before me right now was not Mion. It was someone else entirely.
She had the eyes of a hawk -- eyes that stripped their prey of all freedom.
The intensity of that gaze was staggering.
I felt as though I had no freedom of choice whatsoever.
At last, I managed to squeeze out a single sentence. ...No -- I was made to say it.
``...W-we're friends... right...?@k@vS17/01/110100300. You and me...``
``Friends...?@k@vS17/03/110300274. Ahahahahahah...``
Mion's dry laugh froze me to the bone.
I felt as though she was telling me: those words mean nothing.
And more than that, it sounded like she was saying: friendship is irrelevant -- if you get in the way, you're just another enemy.
``Well, whatever.@k@vS17/03/110300276. Anyway, this is truly the last time we'll talk about this.``
Mion turned the usual corner and headed down the road toward her house.@k Tossing out a casual 'bye-bye' as if nothing had happened.
For a while, I couldn't move from that spot...
...I didn't understand. Was Mion truly a threat after all?
As I stood there rooted in place... the chorus of the higurashi echoed through my ears, same as always.
